title: Doryopteris actinophylla
taxonomy: Doryopteris actinophylla belongs to the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair or tree fern family. This species is classified within the genus Doryopteris, which is a genus of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Doryopteris actinophylla is further divided into the order Polypodiales, the class Polypodiopsida, and the division Pteridophyta. It is also known to be a part of the kingdom Plantae. Its classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships with other plant species.
uses: Doryopteris actinophylla has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant is reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, which may help alleviate fever and reduce inflammation. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and stomach pain. The leaves of the plant are often used to make a tea that is consumed to relieve respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Doryopteris actinophylla.
